calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Martha Daetwyler and Steven Bovarnick for Laurel Scrivani.

Counsel explained circumstances and services provided with respect to sale of the property and litigation with the Tenant to support extraordinary fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Continued To Oct-07-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Verified Petition To Revoke Probate Of Will
Off Calendar Oct-10-2012 Off Calendar

Respondent'S Demurrer To Petition To Revoke Probate Of Will
Off Calendar Oct-04-2012 Off Calendar

Verified Petition To Revoke Probate Of Will
Off Calendar Sep-26-2012 Continued To Oct-10-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Application For Order Authorizing Executor To Complete Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Sep-19-2012 Granted Per Written Order

Ex Parte Application For Order Authorizing Executor To Complete Sale Of Real Property
Off Calendar Sep-12-2012 Continued To Sep-18-12 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 202

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (470 Paris St., San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Aug-30-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Martha Daetwyler for Laurel Scrivani. Timothy Murphy (Objector) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Murphy stated that he has a long standing lease hold interest (19 years), and expressed his concerns about the sale going forward, raised issues with new ownership and his rights. Mr. Murphy also raised the issue of improper notice and jurisdiction. Ms. Daetwyler argued that Mr. Murphy does not have standing to object to sale of the property or to the probate, and addressed the issue of notice. The Court overruled Mr. Murphy's objection, stated that the sale can go forward, that this Court does have jurisdiction, that notice is proper and that Mr. Murphy's rights are governed by ordinance and statute. Both Realtors were present and agreed to proceed with a 5% commission. The Buyer (Howard Su) was also present and waived notice. The sale was confirmed at $283,888.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No additional bond is required.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-28-2012 Granted Per Written Order
